Create content for specific sides of the brain…

Here’s how

when you create content that focuses on the features and the benefits and the tangible tactical things that the client is getting

You’re selling to the right sided brain the analytical part of the brain that makes very well thought out decision decisions

This was solely attract people who are right brain thinkers, who are very analytical who will fight you on price take hours if not days to make decisions and will ultimately Syed with the cheapest option because that is what is most important to them

It’s why all of this work you’re doing to make your offer seem like it has all these bells and whistles ends up, bringing in the brokest leads

Conversely, if you sell to the left side of the human brain by using desired outcomes and story selling wrapped up in emotional analogies, you will begin to whip your viewers into a frenzy of desire and want

And this is powerful because this can be used for good, and this can definitely be used to scam and grift 

So to do this effectively and have a clear idea of what the true desired outcome is for the product that you’re selling

Then you want to lead with that desired outcome, while telling a story that illustrates the path to that desired outcome using your method

Do you want to over exaggerate how easy this was how superior it was to other options and the fact that this method is new indifferent compared to other things out there

Make it relatable by showing as many people as possible who will also gone through this method and achieve this result and also believe that it is superior irresistible new and different?

In doing so you will be selling hope to people who will pay money for more hope

Again, this can be used for good or evil so be careful with this information

Volume is the great equalizer.

Just to run you though this as a real example, with my own content.

I’ve posted 1,644 times on Instagram. 

If you publish 3x videos per week on average, that’s 10.5 years you’ve have to post consistently in order to expect similar results. 

If you publish 5x per week on average, that’s 6.3 years. 

I don’t say this is discourage, actually the opposite.

Hopefully after looking at a few creators, you realize that their results aren’t due to luck, they’re a result of persistence. 

And with enough pig headedness, you can have the same.
All the traits you admire in others, you too have in yourself.

So what’s the solution? 

Unlimited, asynchronous 1:1 coaching.

Offer consistent, weekly support in a group setting.

Then, offer unlimited 1:1 calls… I know, crazy, but hear me out. 

Allocate a time in your calendar. Add a call agenda submission system, and give your clients the ability to book in calls when they MOST need them, as frequently as they desire. 

That’s the big difference. When it’s in the calendar each week, because that’s the time we agreed on arbitrarily, urgency drops. 

But when it’s in the calendar because James is launching his new offer, or Gracie is pressure testing some new landing page copy. Then the calls have purpose and meaning.

There is a direct correlation between my students who lack confidence & how vague and non specific their offer becomes. 

When someone lacks confidence in their ability to provide a specific outcome, they then think that leaving wiggle room in their offer will allow them to get away with not providing that outcome. 

But the issue is, people don’t pay for vague outcomes. They pay for certainty. 

And so ironically, your lack of confidence might be the reason you haven’t had the opportunity to build that confidence by having people choose to do business with you. 

It’s chicken of the egg. 

Either you’re willing to put your money where your mouth is, promise an outcome and then move heaven and earth to make sure your clients achieve it. 

Or you can be vague, make no money. 

Choice is yours.

I’ve worked with thousands of creators, and hundreds of them have asked for my vote of confidence in going for their first hire. Usually an editor, videographer or writer.

Here’s what I always tell them:

View hiring an experimental investment in your business, not a loss of income. 

Will this new person work out? Maybe, I don’t know. 

Will you be a better entrepreneur on the other side of making that initial investment? Absolutely.

So here’s what you can do to mitigate some of the nerves.

Set aside an investment stash, an amount of money you’re willing to see evaporate to test hiring. Maybe it’s $1,000, $10,000 or $100,000.

Set a timeline that gives you an escape hatch but also gives your new hire a chance to prove themselves (2-4 months I find is good)

Go in with the understanding that this will be a step back before it’s a step forward. People talk about hiring like it’s unlocking the secrets of the universe. It’s not, it’s going to suck at first. 

They’re not going to do things the way you want right out the gate. But not because they suck, but because you suck as a leader. 

You’re both investing in this partnership. 

So go in with a set amount of money, a set timeline, and an open mind, and you’ll undoubtedly come away better off. Either because you hired a weapon; or because you learnt a valuable lesson.

That’s business, most people get injured. 

They take a bet that goes south and run out of money. 

They work 16+ hour days and burn themselves out beyond reproach. 

When if they had have focused on longevity, they’d have ended up more successful. 

Now, that’s not to say you can’t work hard. 

You should absolutely work hard. But even elite athletes take recovery days. Even elite athletes periodize their training to manure volume and intensity. 

Even elite athletes understand where their limits are, and that understanding is what creates elite performance on race day. 

Treat yourself like an elite athlete. 

I’ve set some enormous goals for this year.

But I know, if my goals are that high, I need to manage my volume and intensity, just like I do with my training. 

And so, to manage the intensity of my goals, I have simultaneously:

- Decided to quit drinking alcohol for 2026
- Become incredibly protective of my weekends 
- Adopted a “no screens Sundays” plan 
- Created a “slow” morning routine so I actually don’t start work till 10am, but my work hours are now far more intense. 

All in an attempt to give myself the energy to push after my enormous goals. 

Long story short; if you’re an entrepreneur, pushing to build a 1% life, you are a high performer, treat yourself as such.

Create content for specific sides of the brain…

when you create content that focuses on the features and the benefits and the tangible tactical things that the client is getting

The 3 Year Roadmap To Building A Real Online Coaching Business

Most coaches want the $100K months but have no idea what the actual phases look like

Here’s the breakdown
——
YEAR 1: $0 to $10K/mo
Investment: $5K to $10K

Months 1 to 3 you’re learning how software works, how to build an offer and how to post consistently
Months 3 to 6 you’re developing speaking skills, sales ability and business operations solo

Expect to suffer a bit
This is your first grind period
Months 6 to 12 you hit your stride
Posting daily is non negotiable
Find your voice in the DMs

Know your prospects pain points inside and out
Step into longer form content to showcase expertise

Goal: Replace your income and go full time by end of year 1

——
YEAR 2: $10K to $30K/mo
Investment: $10K to $20K in mentorship and better tools

You’re the best kept secret
Clients love the results but lead flow is lower than you want
Time for systems

Organic AND paid traffic not one or the other
Build repeatable systems for lead flow, content, onboarding and fulfillment

Refine your messaging and find the winning pain points
Above all MEASURE and know your numbers

Months 19 to 24 you begin replacing yourself
First 1 to 2 hires in sales and fulfillment

But only if your system is documented and you can lead a team
Goal: Repeatable system with a small team and vision to 2 to 3X volume

——
YEAR 3+: $30K to $100K+/mo
Investment: $20K to $50K in mentorship
$5K to $25K/mo in ads
$5K to $15K/mo in team

This is business now
Data and tracking is life
Without it you’re scaling blind
Decision making is your greatest skill

3 to 5 key YESes and 100 good NOs all year

Your offer must be airtight
Ignore the noise
You think in quarters not months
You plan for the next 2 to 3 years not the next 2 to 3 months

Goal: Happy customers, low churn, predictable lead flow, owned media

And above all 70% PROFIT MARGINS or all this was for an expensive ego trip

Most coaches who are struggling with content are not struggling because their ideas are bad or their production is lacking, they are struggling because they keep abandoning their message before it ever has a chance to land with the right person at the right time.

The instinct to constantly reinvent the strategy every few weeks resets the algorithm, confuses the audience, and kills the compounding effect that consistency creates over time.
People do not need to be taught something new nearly as much as they need to be reminded of what they already know but are not yet acting on.

The coach who shows up and hammers the same core message ten different ways over ninety days will always outperform the coach chasing a new idea every single day hoping something goes viral.
Repetition builds trust and trust is what converts a follower into a client.

Your ideal client needs to see your message multiple times before it lands at the exact moment they are finally ready to act, and if you switched topics last week you will not be there when that moment arrives.
Show up with the same message consistently, say it in different ways, and let the compounding do what one perfect post never could.

Everyone talks about work/life balance like it’s something you achieve once and keep forever.

It’s not.

Balance isn’t static.

It’s a rhythm you have to find over and over again.

Some seasons require you to go all in on building.

Late nights. 
Deep focus. 
Sacrifice.

Other seasons require you to step back completely.

Rest.
Travel. 
Recharge.

The mistake most founders make is trying to split the difference every single day.

Half-working on vacation.

Half-present at dinner while checking Slack.

That’s not balance. That’s just being mediocre at both.

Real balance is knowing when to push and when to pull back.

When to build and when to live.

It’s not about perfection.

It’s about finding your rhythm and honoring it.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Slow down. Reflect. Recharge. 

That’s how you create a life and business that thrives.

I got a confession for you…

AI can write your copy, build your funnels, and launch entire campaigns in minutes.

So execution isn’t the bottleneck anymore.

Taste is.

Taste is the ability to look at something and know it’s not good enough. Without anyone telling you.

You can just feel when something’s off or when it hits.

It’s the photography you choose. The words you cut. The spacing in your slides. The color grade. The shot you reframe because the first angle felt wrong.

That feeling people get when they see your brand? 

That’s taste.

And you develop it by what you consume.

If you’re scrolling mediocre content all day, your output will feel flat.

Study what’s great. Obsess over the small decisions that separate good from iconic.

AI will replace execution.

But it won’t replace the judgment to see what everyone else missed.

Taste is the only advantage that compounds.

Because execution gets commoditized.

Sound judgment never does.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
